page: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9575F processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, the processor includes 16 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AES cryptography, and SHA extensions. The server is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 150 GB of local SSD storage alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations reveal highly polarized performance characteristics. The server achieves top-tier status in single-core operations, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance reaching 3756.97 and single-threaded gzip compression registering at 38.22 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded and system-wide benchmarks show limitations, likely due to the shared CPU allocation. Multi-core stress-ng performance drops to average levels, while PassMark CPU, memory, and database operation scores fall into the bottom 25%. Similarly, Redis SET operations are weak at 4.11 million operations per second, and medium-model LLM inference speeds are poor, processing only 5.5 tokens per second for text generation.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a high memory-to-vCPU density on a shared-resource model, making it an economical option for memory-intensive tasks that do not require sustained, dedicated multi-core compute power. The combination of strong single-threaded performance and generous memory makes it suitable for lightweight caching layers, single-threaded data processing, static web serving, and basic memory-bound utility workloads. It is less suited for heavy multi-threaded databases, complex physics simulations, or large-scale machine learning inference.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 2 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM and 150 GB of local SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it supports AVX-512 and AES instructions. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core execution, highlighted by strong single-threaded compression and decompression rates. However, its shared CPU allocation leads to weaker multi-threaded performance, with PassMark scores and database operations falling into the lower percentiles. This resource profile offers high memory density relative to compute cores, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for memory-intensive but compute-light workloads. It is well-suited for static web serving, lightweight caching, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-16GB is a KVM virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 150 GB SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive, compute-light workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-16GB is a KVM virtual server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 150 GB SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring strong single-core performance and local SSD storage for memory-bound, compute-light workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
150 GB of local SSD storage and 1 public IPv4 address included
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Weaker multi-threaded execution and database operations due to shared CPU allocation
Best suited for static web serving, lightweight caching, and single-threaded processing
